title: Auto-grader scoring spec for core item types (incl. partial credit)
context: Before any question bank can claim to be auto-gradable, a precise, versioned scoring specification must define exactly one deterministic scoring function per item type. Partial credit for multiple-select and matching/ordering items is an open decision that must be standardized before authoring begins, because QTI 3.0 and Moodle express partial credit differently. The spec must also define the self-test contract (key→100%, wrong→0, partial→defined value) that the CI harness will enforce. Without this spec the validator (quiz-bank-open-validator-006) and all exporters cannot be correctly implemented.
objective: Specify the deterministic scoring function for each supported item type, standardize the partial-credit formula, define the self-test contract, and provide a mapping note for QTI 3.0 and Moodle where their native models differ.
acceptanceCriteria: One deterministic scoring function is specified per item type: single-select (exact id match), multiple-select (standardized partial-credit formula, documented), true/false (exact), numeric (value within tolerance with optional unit normalization), short-text (Unicode-normalized/trimmed/case-folded match against acceptedAnswers), cloze (per-blank scoring), matching/ordering (per-pair or Kendall-style partial credit, documented).

Partial-credit formula for multiple-select and matching/ordering is standardized with a single chosen formula documented, including a mapping note for QTI 3.0 and Moodle flagging where their native models differ or cannot express the formula.
The scoring spec is versioned with a scoringSpecVersion field and recorded in specVersions.
Self-test contract is defined: published key scores 100%; at least one declared wrong answer per item scores 0; partial-credit items score the defined partial value for defined partial responses.
Document is CC-BY-4.0 licensed and committed to the project repo.
resources: quiz-bank-open-model-001 (canonical item model — defines scoring field shapes)
Moodle GIFT and Moodle XML import format docs (Moodle 4.x partial-credit rules)
output: A versioned scoring specification document committed to the project repo, covering all supported item types with scoring functions, the standardized partial-credit formula, the self-test contract, and a QTI 3.0 / Moodle mapping note.
